report_name: Ameriprise Financial Discloses Second Data Breach in Six Months, Affecting Nearly 48,000 Individuals

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: Allegedly linked to the data breach at Ameriprise Financial. The group was said to have claimed responsibility in now-dropped lawsuits and threatened to release over 200GB of exfiltrated internal data.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for subsequent sale or extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The core of the attack involved unauthorized access to and theft of data from Ameriprise Financial's stored files, which could have been a file server, cloud storage, or database.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor allegedly exfiltrated over 200GB of data. This technique is consistent with the known TTPs of the allegedly responsible group, ShinyHunters, who often exfiltrate large volumes of data to sell.

identities.name: Ameriprise Financial
identities.description: A U.S. financial services company that suffered a data breach exposing the personal and financial information of 47,876 individuals.
